growth_records.medium: DM9 + 25 mM acetate (aerated spent S. ovata DM9 medium, minimal processing)

sec.method: peak volumetric PHB synthesis rate over 32-h window

growth_records.cite: Stefano Cestellos-Blanco et al. Production of PHB From CO2-Derived Acetate With Minimal Processing Assessed for Space Biomanufacturing. Frontiers in Microbiology (2021). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2021.700010. [PMC8355900]
growth_records.snippet: we calculated a PHB generation rate of 12.6 mg L-1 hour-1 with an overall 11.06% carbon yield from acetate ... acetate consumption is complete within ~24 h ... The productivity of C. basilensis in this study of 12.54 mg PHB L-1 hr-1
growth_records.notes: Aerobic acetate-to-PHB conversion. PHB productivity reported both as 12.6 mg/L/h and precisely 12.54 mg PHB/L/h (peak rate over 32-h growth window). Carbon yield acetate-C->PHB-C = 11.06%. Acetate uptake derived: 25 mM consumed within ~24 h -> 25/24 = 1.04 mmol/L/h (volumetric, not biomass-specific; text says 'acetate consumption complete within ~24 h'). PHB degrades after acetate exhaustion (cells consume PHB reserves). Grown at 30 C, pH 7.2 (starter DM9+25 mM sodium acetate conditions). DM9 full composition in Supplementary Table 1 (not in full text); only acetate concentration stated in text -> medium_composition_stated false. Reference comparison: Cupriavidus reported elsewhere up to 1.85 g PHB/L/h (Atlic et al. 2011) — cited from another paper, NOT this work's measurement.
